channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Congolese church leaders meet Rwanda-backed rebels in the east in latest push for peace
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Congo’s largest church organizations met Wednesday with Rwanda-backed rebels in the country’s east in the latest push for peace and dialogue after weekslong fighting that saw the rebels seize the region’s largest city of Goma.
item.dc:creator: RUTH ALONGA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Egypt reportedly releases details on plan to rebuild Gaza; there's no mention of 'cooperation' with the US
item.description: Egyptian sources have reportedly revealed initial details of a proposal for the reconstruction of the Gaza Strip, but it appears to omit plans to "cooperate" with the U.S. or Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard sworn in as top intelligence chief in Oval Office
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard was sworn in as director of national intelligence on Wednesday in the Oval Office, hours after the Senate confirmed her. Attorney General Pam Bondi administered the oath of office to Gabbard, who Trump called “an American of extraordinary courage and patriotism.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Things to know about Russia's release of American Marc Fogel and the impact on ending Ukraine war
item.description: The U.S. and Russia agreed to a prisoner swap involving Marc Fogel, an American schoolteacher who the Biden administration had deemed wrongfully detained by Russia, in a diplomatic move that the White House said could move forward negotiations to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: White House acknowledges disagreement with Jordan over relocating Palestinians
item.description: The White House on Wednesday acknowledged disagreement between President Trump and King Abdullah II of Jordon over Trump's pitch to relocate Palestinians from the Gaza Strip while the U.S. takes over the territory.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Austrian far-right leader's efforts to form a new government collapse
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Austrian far-right leader Herbert Kickl's efforts to form a coalition government with a conservative party collapsed in mutual recriminations on Wednesday, more than four months after his party won a national election.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel threatens 'all hell will break loose' on Hamas in latest Gaza ceasefire crisis
item.description: Israel’s defense minister says “all hell will break loose” if Hamas does not release three hostages as planned this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Tia Goldenberg And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Thousands in Taiwan and China celebrate the Lantern Festival with high hopes and rice dumplings
item.description: NEW TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Thousands in Taiwan and China celebrated the Lantern Festival on Wednesday by releasing paper lanterns into the night sky, visiting light installations and snacking on glutinous rice dumplings.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Syria's interim foreign minister says Russia-Iran ties to Assad are an open wound after war
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Syria's relations with Iran and Russia remain an open wound for its people after those nations backed autocratic President Bashar Assad during the long civil war, the country's new, interim foreign minister said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Egypt and Qatar are working to salvage the Gaza ceasefire deal
item.description: Egyptian and Qatari mediators were working to salvage the ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas on Wednesday, which risks being derailed because of a dispute between the two sides, according to Egypt’s state-run Al-Qahera News TV, which is close to the country's security agencies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A joke petition asks for signers to help Denmark buy California as Trump eyes Greenland
item.description: Is California for sale? A lighthearted petition to buy the state of California for Denmark prompted more than 200,000 signatures by Wednesday in response to President Donald Trump’s talk about taking control of Greenland, a vast and mineral-rich Arctic island.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Too few tents entering Gaza threatens the truce. Here’s what’s happening
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Three weeks into the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas, the number of tents and temporary homes entering Gaza risks falling short of the goals set for the deal's first phase.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US releasing Russian prisoner Alexander Vinnik in Marc Fogel exchange, official says
item.description: The United States is releasing Russian prisoner Alexander Vinnik as part of the deal to secure Marc Fogel's freedom, a Trump administration official said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN halts humanitarian work in Yemen's Houthi stronghold after staff detentions
item.description: The United Nations has suspended humanitarian operations in northern Saada after rebels detained eight more U.N. staffers, during one of the world's worst humanitarian crises.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fogel release a sign of Trump, Putin's 'great friendship': Witkoff
item.description: Trump and Putin had a "great friendship, and I think now it's going to continue," special envoy Steve Witkoff said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: UN rights office estimates up to 1,400 killed in crackdown on protests in Bangladesh
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The U.N. human rights office on Wednesday estimated that up to 1,400 people may have been killed in Bangladesh over three weeks last summer in a crackdown on student-led protests against the now-ousted former prime minister.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump readies matching tariffs on trade partners, possibly setting up a major economic showdown
item.description: President Donald Trump could sign an order as soon as Wednesday to require that U.S. tariffs on imports match the tax rates charged by other countries.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope rebukes Trump administration over immigrant deportations and appears to aim directly at Vance
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis issued a major rebuke Tuesday to the Trump administration’s plans for mass deportations of migrants, warning that the forceful removal of people purely because of their illegal status deprives them of their inherent dignity and “will end badly.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US aid freeze paralyzes NGOs working to help millions of internally displaced people in Somalia
item.description: MOGADISHU, Somalia (AP) — In a desolate makeshift camp on the fringes of Somalia's capital, tens of thousands of internally displaced people sit under the baking sun not sure if they can have access to food rations and medication following U.S.
item.dc:creator: OMAR FARUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN food agency worker dies in a Yemeni prison after being detained by Houthi rebels
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The United Nations World Food Program said Tuesday one of its staffers has died in a Yemeni prison three weeks after his detention by Houthi rebels. The announcement came a day after the U.N.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Families bid farewell to victims of Guatemala bus crash
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO LOS OCOTES, Guatemala (AP) — Hours before dawn, Julio Arrivillaga and Catalina Pérez Molina boarded a bus with other residents in the center of this humble village for what should have been an hour-long ride to Guatemala’s capital.
item.dc:creator: SONIA PÉREZ D., Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American Marc Fogel released from Russian custody
item.description: An American teacher detained by Russia is heading back to American soil, the Trump administration announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli cabinet backs Trump’s demand for Hamas to release all hostages by Saturday deadline
item.description: Israel’s security cabinet supports President Trump’s demand that Hamas release all of its hostages by noon on Saturday, an Israeli official told Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he may withhold aid to Jordan, Egypt if they don’t take in Palestinians
item.description: President Trump said Monday he would consider withholding aid to Jordan and Egypt if they do not agree to take in Palestinians as part of a plan for the U.S. to take over and develop the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Venezuelan planes sent to US for deportation flights return to country with nearly 200 deportees
item.description: Two planes sent by Venezuela returned to the country from El Paso, Texas, on Monday with nearly 200 Venezuelans who were in the U.S. illegally.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ecuador's presidential election goes to runoff between conservative incumbent, leftist lawyer
item.description: Ecuador will choose its next president in a runoff election in April between conservative incumbent Daniel Noboa and leftist lawyer Luisa González. Crime is a major issue for voters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Marine identified as victim of surveillance flight that crashed in Philippines
item.description: Military officials identified a U.S. Marine who died in a plane crash in the Philippines last week as Sgt. Jacob M. Durham, a California native.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 7.6 magnitude earthquake shakes the Caribbean, some islands urge residents to leave coastlines
item.description: A magnitude-7.6 earthquake shook the Caribbean Sea south of the Cayman Islands Saturday, according to the U.S. Geological Survey. Several islands and countries urged people near the coastline to move inland but authorities in most places later lifted the tsunami alerts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Egypt lobbies against Trump plan to empty Gaza of Palestinians as Israel prepares for it
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel says it has begun preparations for the departure of Palestinians from Gaza despite international rejection of President Donald Trump’s plan to empty the territory of its population.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: AI, scientists unite to decipher old scrolls charred by Vesuvius volcano
item.description: Hundreds of papyrus scrolls were found in the 1750s in the remains of a lavish villa at the Roman town of Herculaneum.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation
